description: Chapter 1 of The Moon's Ledger. A watched approach to the Circus way-house. One sentry stands between Wren and the door — slip past on Stealth, or open with a Sneak Attack from the dark.
customIntroduction: Two years of running, and the Circus finally moved. Word came through a fence in Harrowgate: the troupe keeps a way-house out past the toll road, and in its ledger is a page with Wren's old name on it — and the contract she walked away from. She means to take that page back before they collect what it says she owes. The way-house squats at the end of a long, open approach. A lantern burns by the door. Between her and it stands a single sentry, half-asleep on his feet, not yet aware that tonight is different.
customContext: MOON'S LEDGER — CHAPTER 1 of 4. STEALTH + AMBUSH TUTORIAL for Wren Allowyn (Halfling Rogue, Expertise in Stealth). One bandit sentry guards the approach; he begins HIDDEN to the player only in the sense that the player likewise can HIDE and approach unseen. Teach the loop: HIDE (Wren has Cunning-Action-grade stealth), close the distance using cover, and open with a Sneak Attack for Advantage + bonus dice — or try to slip past entirely. The sentry is a routine fighter, not a boss; reward the quiet approach. On his defeat the way-house door is open and the chapter ends. Keep it tense and low-lethality — this is the opening beat of a heist, not a pitched battle.
objective: Reach the way-house door. Deal with the sentry — quietly, if you can.

then.message: Open on the long, lantern-lit approach to the Circus way-house. One sentry, half-asleep, between Wren and the door. Remind her — in the fiction — that she is at her best unseen: she can HIDE and close under cover for a Sneak Attack, or try to slip past. One short paragraph, then hand control to the player.
